questionHtml:
Iron is a stronger reducing agent than silver.

What is correct when this voltaic cell is in operation?

meta.title: Iron is a stronger reducing agent than silver. What is correct when this voltaic cell is in operation?
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 3. What are the mechanisms of chemical change?

Reactivity 3.2.6—A primary (voltaic) cell is an electrochemical cell that converts energy from spontaneous redox reactions to electrical energy. Explain the direction of electron flow from anode to cathode in the external circuit, and ion movement across the salt bridge.
